title: House Bed (Twin)
summary: A twin bed with stick-built 2x3 house-shaped end frames and a 2x2 ridge beam tying the peaks together — an open frame silhouette, not board-and-batten wall siding.
description:
Each house-shaped end is its own small frame: two 2x3 legs, four horizontal 2x3 cross pieces at different heights, and two angled roof pieces meeting at a peak — one roof piece slightly longer than the other since they overlap at the ridge rather than mitering to a perfect point. Build both ends identically and set them aside before joining anything lengthwise.

Connect the two ends with the long 2x3 side rails at mattress height, a 2x3 back top tie near the top of the back wall, and a **2x2 ridge beam running peak to peak** — this beam is what keeps the two house shapes from racking apart sideways, so don't skip fastening it solidly at both ends.

The slat system is twelve 2x3 (or 1x3) slats running across the frame, held in place along both long edges by 1x2 slat trim — the trim is what stops the slats from creeping sideways under a moving sleeper, so run it the full length on both sides.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Longest point measurement, one end cut at 45°.
Longest point measurement, one end cut at 45°.

steps.title: Build the two house-shaped ends
steps.body: Build two identical end frames: two legs, four horizontal cross pieces at their marked heights, and the two angled roof pieces meeting at the peak.

steps.title: Connect the ends
steps.body: Join the two end frames with the side rails, the back top tie, and the ridge beam running peak to peak — fasten the ridge beam solidly at both ends.

steps.title: Add the slats and trim
steps.body: Lay the twelve slats across the frame, spaced evenly, then nail the slat trim along both long edges to keep them from creeping sideways.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Twin house bed with stick-built gable ends
